# Stemmer corpus provenance

These fixtures are the **Pagefind query-stemmer oracle**, not this package's
own output. Pagefind stems queries at runtime with the Rust crate
`pagefind_stem`; the `expected-stems.txt` files are that crate's output over
`words.txt`, and the stemmer parity tests assert the PHP stemmer reproduces
them exactly — byte-for-byte, for every language in `Stemmer::LANGUAGE_MAP`.
This is what makes the parity test meaningful — it compares the indexer to
Pagefind, not to itself.

## Targeted Pagefind release

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Pagefind version | **1.5.0** (the version the scolta packages bundle/serve) |
| Stemmer crate (from Pagefind 1.5.0 `Cargo.lock`) | `pagefind_stem` **1.0.0** |
| crates.io checksum | `8dfa810b158f3ac364e5acd43ca4a6020a6e729d40c15ce1bed1d911237a52e5` |
| Algorithm revision | modern Snowball (post-3.0 / 2024): `added`→`add`, `organic`→`organic`, `geologist`→`geolog`, `organize`→`organiz` |
| Reproduced by | the vendored stemmers in `src/Index/Snowball/` (snowball commit `019c1bd`, see PROVENANCE.md there) |

Generated by `tools/stemmer-golden` (pins `pagefind_stem = "=1.0.0"`). To
re-target a newer Pagefind, follow `tools/stemmer-golden/README.md` and update
the table + checksums below.

## Word lists

- **en, fr, de, es, ru**: inherited unchanged from the scolta-python fixture
  set (hashes identical to `scolta-python/tests/fixtures/stemmer-corpus/`).
- **ca, da, fi, it, nl, no, pt, ro, sv**: from the snowball project's own test
  vocabularies, <https://github.com/snowballstem/snowball-data> at commit
  `a0ec0d0a2839ec885878868de20fcb63209d92b0` (`<language>/voc.txt`,
  lowercased with `mb_strtolower` and deduplicated). snowball-data is
  BSD-3-Clause by default; the **catalan, finnish, and italian** lists
  additionally contain Wikipedia-derived words licensed
  [CC BY-SA 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/), per each
  language's `COPYING` file in that repository.

## sha256 manifest (drift guard)

`StemmerCorpusProvenanceTest` asserts the committed fixtures still hash to
these values. A mismatch means the corpus was regenerated (e.g. against a new
Pagefind stemmer) — update this manifest and the targeted-version table in the
same commit so the move is explicit, never silent.

| lang | words.txt | expected-stems.txt |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ca | `31b2ea26352e2bb68f4069e2c84dc4b5abfe4bd3857f4e45eb8244ae3a9732f1` | `0598b1929582f9778231245c0ad4bfa8cfb2d3f29ac050f8f0403f68a76b41e8` |
